qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Memory exhaustion via unbounded width in numeric sequence padding
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes exponential combinatorial expansion from multiple repeated numeric brace ranges (e.g., {0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99} producing 10 billion combinations). VULN-001 describes a different root cause: unbounded padding width in a single numeric range ({0[...50000 zeros...]..1}) causing oversized individual strings via new Array(need+1).join('0'). The vulnerable code paths differ: advisory root cause is the combinatorial expansion engine generating all result combinations; VULN-001 root cause is the width calculation at line 171 and padding generation at lines 193-203. Same function and same general vulnerability class (DoS in brace expansion), but different specific mechanisms and code paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-7h2j-956f-4vf2) describes uncontrolled resource consumption from exponential combinatorial expansion when multiple numeric brace ranges like {0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99} are combined, producing 10 billion results eagerly and synchronously. The only qualifying finding (VULN-001) describes a different vulnerability: memory exhaustion via unbounded padding width in a single numeric range, where leading zeros control the width parameter at src/index.ts:171 and oversized padding strings are generated at src/index.ts:193-203. These are distinct root causes with different attack vectors, different vulnerable code paths, and different resource consumption mechanisms (number of results vs. size of individual strings).
evidence: Advisory root cause: exponential combinatorial expansion from multiple repeated brace ranges {0..99}{0..99}... producing 10^10 combinations
Advisory PoC pattern: {0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99} causing heap out of memory
VULN-001 root cause: unbounded width from leading zeros in a single numeric range, padding string generation
VULN-001 PoC pattern: {0[...50000 zeros...]..1} creating width=50001, exploiting new Array(need+1).join('0')
Different vulnerable code paths: advisory targets the combinatorial expansion engine; VULN-001 targets width calculation (line 171) and padding generation (lines 193-203)
